does: This paper introduces Auto-Rubric as Reward (ARR), a framework that automatically generates explicit, multidimensional evaluation criteria for aligning multimodal generative models with human preferences. Rather than relying on implicit reward functions or scalar labels, ARR uses a vision-language model to extract and externalize rubrics—decomposed quality dimensions—before performing pairwise comparisons. The method translates holistic preferences into independently verifiable criteria that constrain evaluation, demonstrating substantial reductions in evaluation biases such as positional bias while maintaining reliability and scalability.
connects: This work builds on recent Rubrics-as-Reward approaches that aim to recover compositional structure from human judgments, addressing known limitations of standard RLHF methods that collapse nuanced preferences into opaque scalar rewards or pairwise labels. It extends prior research on reward modeling and human alignment by tackling the core challenge of generating high-quality, scalable rubrics without extensive manual annotation. The paper also connects to broader work on interpretability in reward functions, bias mitigation in preference learning, and the use of vision-language models for zero-shot or few-shot evaluation and reasoning tasks.
enables: This framework opens pathways for more transparent and auditable reward modeling in multimodal generation tasks, enabling clearer human oversight of AI alignment processes. By externalizing preference structure as interpretable criteria, ARR could facilitate better reward hacking detection and mitigation, supporting safer deployment of generative models. Future work could extend automatic rubric generation to other domains, develop methods for hierarchical or compositional rubrics, integrate rubric-based rewards more directly into RLHF training loops, and explore how explicit criteria improve sample efficiency and generalization of learned preference models across diverse evaluation contexts.
